top_gaps.label: Preserve saved libraries across data-model upgrades
top_gaps.why: This matters because the plan never defines migration/backfill behavior, so a schema revision could strand or silently drop existing user collections and My Data.

top_gaps.label: Sync libraries/settings consistently and merge duplicates
top_gaps.why: This matters because the plan names merge logic, but not duplicate detection or synced-settings consistency, which creates real cross-device drift and duplicate-record risk.

top_gaps.label: Validate discovery with rubric and hard-fail integrity
top_gaps.why: This matters because the plan resolves real shows, but it never defines a quality gate for taste alignment, surprise, or discovery regressions before they reach users.

top_gaps.label: Retry malformed mention output once, then fallback
top_gaps.why: This matters because Ask depends on structured mentions for follow-on navigation, and a parser miss without an Ask-specific fallback can break the mentioned-shows experience.

top_gaps.label: Deliver surprising but defensible taste-aligned recommendations
top_gaps.why: This matters because the plan covers concept grounding, but without an explicit surprise-without-betrayal bar the product can devolve into safe, generic recommendations.

narrative.overall_posture: The plan is structurally strong and implementation-ready across most of the denominator, but it is not fully closed. It covers the app shell, collection flows, detail page, AI surface architecture, and export path well, while leaving a small set of high-leverage behavioral contracts underspecified.
narrative.strength_clusters: The strongest coverage is in Benchmark Runtime & Isolation, App Navigation & Discover Shell, and Person Detail, all of which are fully covered. Collection Home & Search and Show Detail & Relationship UX are also strong because the plan gives concrete UI structure, persistence hooks, and route/API boundaries rather than vague feature bullets.
narrative.weakness_clusters: The gaps are not random. They cluster in three places: long-term data lifecycle rules inside Collection Data & Persistence, AI quality contracts in Ask plus Concepts/Alchemy, and the last layer of settings/state persistence. In other words, the plan is strongest on surface implementation and weakest on durability and quality-governance semantics.
narrative.risk_assessment: If this plan were executed as-is, the first stakeholder-visible problems would likely be subtle rather than obvious: schema changes could jeopardize existing libraries, cross-device sync could duplicate or drift records, and AI discovery could feel structurally correct but too generic or brittle when structured mention parsing fails. QA would notice these as edge-case regressions around upgrades, synchronization, and recommendation quality rather than missing screens.
narrative.remediation_guidance: The remaining planning work is mostly about hardening contracts, not inventing new features. The plan needs explicit migration/versioning strategy for persisted user libraries, clearer sync and duplicate-resolution rules for both shows and settings, Ask-specific structured-output fallback behavior, and a written quality bar for concept generation and recommendation outputs so AI behavior is testable rather than aspirational.

requirements.label: Preserve saved libraries across data-model upgrades
requirements.gap: The plan never describes versioned migration or backfill work to carry existing saved libraries and My Data forward across schema changes.

requirements.label: Persist synced settings, local settings, UI state
requirements.gap: It covers synced settings plus a few local flags, but it omits persisted last-selected filter state and the broader split between synced settings, local settings, and UI state.

requirements.label: Render poster, title, and My Data badges
Provide empty-library and empty-filter states
Search by title or keywords
Use poster grid with collection markers
Auto-open Search when setting is enabled
requirements.gap: It defines the setting and search-page autofocus, but it does not explicitly route app launch into Search when enabled.

requirements.label: Keep Search non-AI in tone
Preserve Show Detail narrative section order
Prioritize motion-rich header with graceful fallback
Surface year, runtime/seasons, and community score early
Place status/interest controls in toolbar
Auto-save unsaved tagged show as Later/Interested
Auto-save unsaved rated show as Done

Include traditional recommendations strand

requirements.gap: The section order is concrete, but the plan does not describe any mechanism to keep the page powerful without becoming visually overwhelming.

requirements.label: Provide conversational Ask chat interface
Answer directly with confident, spoiler-safe recommendations
Show horizontal mentioned-shows strip from chat
Open Detail from mentions or Search fallback
Show six random starter prompts with refresh

requirements.evidence: 7.1 Conversation summarization prompt; 7.2 Ask Chat Session

requirements.label: Seed Ask-about-show sessions with show handoff
Emit `commentary` plus exact `showList` contract
Retry malformed mention output once, then fallback
requirements.gap: The plan specifies retry-on-parse-fail generically, but it does not tie that fallback explicitly to Ask mention parsing.

requirements.label: Redirect Ask back into TV/movie domain
requirements.gap: The shared prompt stays in TV and movies, but the plan does not specify a redirect-back behavior for off-domain Ask queries.

requirements.label: Treat concepts as taste ingredients, not genres
requirements.source: supporting_docs/concept_system.md > 1. What a Concept Is (User Definition)

requirements.label: Return bullet-only, 1-3 word, non-generic concepts
Order concepts by strongest aha and varied axes
requirements.gap: The plan defines format and count, but it does not require strongest-first ordering or varied concept axes.

requirements.label: Require concept selection and guide ingredient picking
requirements.gap: The flow requires concept selection, but it does not include the ingredient-picking guidance or empty-state nudges from the spec.

requirements.label: Return exactly five Explore Similar recommendations
Support full Alchemy loop with chaining
Clear downstream results when inputs change
Generate shared multi-show concepts with larger option pool
requirements.gap: It enforces shared multi-show concepts, but it does not call for a larger option pool than single-show generation.

requirements.label: Cite selected concepts in concise recommendation reasons
Deliver surprising but defensible taste-aligned recommendations
requirements.gap: The plan grounds recommendations in concepts and library context, but it does not set an explicit surprise-without-betrayal quality bar.

requirements.label: Keep one consistent AI persona across surfaces
Enforce shared AI guardrails across all surfaces
Make AI warm, joyful, and light in critique
Structure Scoop as personal taste mini-review
Keep Ask brisk and dialogue-like by default
Feed AI the right surface-specific context inputs
Validate discovery with rubric and hard-fail integrity
requirements.gap: It enforces real-show resolution, but it does not define the discovery scoring rubric or a validation gate for taste quality.

requirements.label: Show person gallery, name, and bio

Open Show Detail from selected credit
Include font size and Search-on-launch settings

requirements.gap: The plan covers username and model settings, but API keys are environment-configured displays rather than safe configurable settings.
